examples.title: Pass-by-Value: Rebinding vs Mutation
examples.description: Shows that rebinding a parameter to a new object does not affect the caller, while mutating the object does.
examples.pseudocode:
define class Counter with field n
make Counter c with n = 0
call bump(c) which sets c.n = 5
observe caller sees n = 5 (same object mutated)
call rebind(c) which sets c = new Counter(100)
observe caller still sees n = 5 (parameter rebind doesn't escape)

examples.title: Avoiding Aliasing with Arrays
examples.description: Demonstrates aliasing via assignment and how to avoid it with copying.
examples.pseudocode:
create array a = [1,2,3]
let b = a (alias)
set b[0] = 9 => a[0] is now 9
to avoid: let c = copy of a
set c[1] = 99 => a unchanged

examples.title: Equality vs Identity with Strings
examples.description: Highlights the difference between == and equals when comparing Strings.
examples.pseudocode:
make s1 = new String("hi")
make s2 = new String("hi")
identity check (s1 == s2) is false
content check (s1.equals(s2)) is true
